prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial, as represented by the sampled frames, begins with a dark, low-contrast image. A large blurred word or logo appears in the center-left area, but it is not legible from the frame. A “Nostalgic X’er” watermark is visible at the lower right.

By the final frame, the image has changed to a clear product display. On a plain dark background, a white bottle stands on the left and a rectangular product box sits to its right. Both packages are labeled “TRIACTIN,” with the box reading “TRIACTIN chewable tablets.” Large text to the upper right of the bottle states: “Helps Stop Excess Acid Upset.” The “Nostalgic X’er” watermark remains in the lower right corner. The overall presentation is monochrome, with the product and text brightly lit against the darker backdrop.
